input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

compressed_segments.text: [0-2] A static logo displays "RADA CUTLERY" in large, dark blue serif text arched over a silver knife with a cylindrical handle, positioned above a ribbon banner reading "American Made Cutlery" on a white background. [2-4] The scene transitions to a medium shot of a chef wearing a white coat and black hat standing in a kitchen with wooden cabinets and stainless steel appliances. He is behind a counter featuring a wooden cutting board with a baguette, butter, and condiment bottles. [4-6] The chef speaks while facing the camera, with the kitchen background and counter items remaining static. [6-8] He gestures with both hands outward and back as white text appears at the bottom: "(This recipe can be found at the end of the video.)". [12-14] The camera cuts to a close-up of the wooden cutting board, removing previous subtitle text, showing the chef in a white coat and clear plastic gloves gesturing with a knife over the baguette; butter and spice jars are on the right. [14-16] The chef slices the tip of the baguette with the knife in his right hand, stabilizing the bread with his left. [16-18] The tip is finished slicing, leaving a small, round piece

[126-130] A chef wearing a white coat and black hat holds a small jar with a red label in his left hand. He uses a metal spoon in his right hand to scoop a white condiment and transfers it into a clear glass bowl situated on a wooden cutting board. The bowl already contains a mixture of brown sauce and yellow mustard. [130-132] Once the white substance is added, the chef sets the jar down on the counter near a bottle of ketchup and other spice containers. [132-134] He then proceeds to stir the contents of the glass bowl with the metal spoon, blending the brown sauce, yellow mustard, and white condiment together in a circular motion to combine the ingredients.

[134-142] A chef wearing a white coat and black hat stands behind a counter, stirring a reddish-brown mixture in a clear glass bowl placed on a wooden cutting board. He uses a metal spoon in his right hand to blend the ingredients with circular motions while holding the bowl's rim with his left hand. To his right, a ketchup bottle and spice jars sit stationary on the granite countertop near wooden cabinets and a microwave. The chef maintains this mixing action throughout the segment.

[20-22] The chef completes slicing a second baguette round, resting it beside the first on a wooden board; his gloved hands hold a knife (right) and stabilize the loaf (left), with butter and spice jars on the counter's right edge. [142-144] The chef in a white coat and black hat stirs a reddish-brown mixture in a clear glass bowl on the board with a spoon in his right hand, while ketchup and spice jars stay stationary on the right. [144-146] A cross-fade transition shows the chef gesturing with open hands over the board holding toasted baguette slices, with the sauce bowl to his right and a bowl of raw ground meat appearing on the left. [146-148] He points his right index finger at the toasted slices, keeping the meat bowl left and sauce bowl right, while his left hand rests near the board's edge.

[150-154] The chef, wearing a white coat and black hat, places a toasted baguette slice back onto a wooden cutting board and gestures over the arranged slices with his right hand. A glass bowl of raw meat and a sauce bowl sit stationary on the counter. [154-156] He reaches his right hand toward the glass bowl containing raw ground meat on the left side. [156-160] The chef picks up a small white tub, places it near the meat bowl, and uses a utensil in his right hand to scoop a white substance, stabilizing the tub with his left hand to prepare applying the spread to the toasted bread.

The white text overlay at the bottom reading '(This recipe can be found at the end of the video.)' remains visible on the screen. The surrounding kitchen environment and ingredients stay static.

[10-12] The chef extends his left arm to rest his hand near the cluster of bottles and jars on the right side of the counter. His right hand stays positioned on the cutting board near the bread. The white subtitle text remains visible at the bottom of the frame.

[148-150] The chef in the white coat and black hat uses his right hand to pick up a slice of toasted baguette from the wooden cutting board. His left hand hovers near the bread as he lifts the piece, while the bowls of raw meat and sauce remain stationary on the counter.

[160-162] The chef in a white coat and black hat picks up a slice of toasted baguette with his left hand. He uses a utensil in his right hand to spread a white substance, taken from a small tub, onto the bread slice. The other bread slices remain on the wooden cutting board.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

compressed_segments.text: [0-2] A static logo displays "RADA CUTLERY" in large, dark blue serif text arched over a silver knife with a cylindrical handle, positioned above a ribbon banner reading "American Made Cutlery" on a white background. [2-4] The scene transitions to a medium shot of a chef wearing a white coat and black hat standing in a kitchen with wooden cabinets and stainless steel appliances. He is behind a counter featuring a wooden cutting board with a baguette, butter, and condiment bottles. [4-6] The chef speaks while facing the camera, with the kitchen background and counter items remaining static. [6-8] He gestures with both hands outward and back as white text appears at the bottom: "(This recipe can be found at the end of the video.)". [12-14] The camera cuts to a close-up of the wooden cutting board, removing previous subtitle text, showing the chef in a white coat and clear plastic gloves gesturing with a knife over the baguette; butter and spice jars are on the right. [14-16] The chef slices the tip of the baguette with the knife in his right hand, stabilizing the bread with his left. [16-18] The tip is finished slicing, leaving a small, round piece

[78-84] A person in a white coat with black buttons and clear gloves stirs a yellowish garlic mixture in a silver saucepan on a black stovetop. The right hand uses a utensil while the left hand grasps the metal handle. [84-86] The scene shifts to a kitchen island where a chef in a white coat and black hat stands behind a wooden cutting board. He sprinkles green herbs into the silver pot next to sliced bread, then stirs with a spoon. Wooden cabinets and a stainless steel microwave are in the background.

[86-94] A chef wearing a white coat and black hat stands at a kitchen island, stirring a mixture in a silver pot with a green spatula. Sliced bread rests on a wooden cutting board to his left, while bottles of oil and spices sit on the counter to his right against a backdrop of wooden cabinets. He continues stirring the mixture in the silver pot using the green spatula held in his right hand. Simultaneously, his left hand reaches toward the sliced bread pieces resting on the wooden cutting board. A close-up shot reveals a gloved hand holding a slice of baguette topped with garlic and oil mixture above the stainless steel pot, while the other hand stirs the remaining contents inside the pot with the green spatula.

From [94-98], a gloved hand uses a bright green silicone brush to spread a garlic oil mixture from a stainless steel pot onto slices of baguette. The camera zooms out to reveal a chef in a white coat and black hat working at a counter with a wooden cutting board and a baking sheet of coated bread. By [98-100], the chef lifts the baking sheet filled with the prepared garlic bread slices. Finally, from [100-102], the chef turns right and walks toward a stainless steel oven and microwave unit, carrying the tray to bake the bread, passing bottles of oil and spices on the counter.

From 102-108s, a chef wearing a white coat with black cuffs and a black hat bends forward at the waist to open a black oven door situated beneath a stainless steel microwave. He places a baking sheet filled with prepared bread slices inside the oven cavity on the rack. After sliding the tray in, he closes the black oven door while remaining slightly bent forward. From 108-110s, the chef stands upright and faces the camera. He gestures with his right hand while his left hand rests near bottles on the counter. The stainless steel microwave and wooden cabinets remain visible in the background throughout the sequence.

recent_thinks: [8-10] The chef places both hands onto the baguette resting on the wooden cutting board, ceasing previous gestures. The white text overlay at the bottom reading '(This recipe can be found at the end of the video.)' remains visible on the screen. The surrounding kitchen environment and ingredients stay static.

[10-12] The chef extends his left arm to rest his hand near the cluster of bottles and jars on the right side of the counter. His right hand stays positioned on the cutting board near the bread. The white subtitle text remains visible at the bottom of the frame.

[20-22] The chef completes slicing a second round piece from the baguette, which now rests beside the first slice on the wooden board. His gloved hands maintain their positions, with the right hand holding the knife and the left stabilizing the remaining loaf, while butter and spice jars stay visible on the counter's right edge.

[110-112] The chef in a white coat and black hat reaches for a bottle of brown liquid on the counter with his right hand. He lifts the bottle and holds it with both hands over a small glass bowl placed on the wooden cutting board.

[112-114] The chef in a white coat and black hat holds a brown squeeze bottle with both hands over a small glass bowl on the wooden cutting board. He unscrews the cap and begins squeezing the bottle to dispense a dark brown sauce into the clear bowl.

samples.output: <think>The chef in a white coat holds a brown squeeze bottle with both hands over a small glass bowl on a wooden cutting board. He tilts the bottle downward and squeezes it, dispensing a dark brown sauce into the clear bowl. Stainless steel appliances and wooden cabinets are visible in the background.</think><action>silent</action>

He is behind a counter featuring a wooden cutting board with a baguette, butter, and condiment bottles. [4-6] The chef speaks while facing the camera, with the kitchen background and counter items remaining static. [6-8] He gestures with both hands outward and back as white text appears at the bottom: "(This recipe can be found at the end of the video.)". [12-14] The camera cuts to a close-up of the wooden cutting board, removing previous subtitle text, showing the chef in a white coat and clear plastic gloves gesturing with a knife over the baguette; butter and spice jars are on the right. [142-144] The chef in a white coat and black hat stirs a reddish-brown mixture in a clear glass bowl on the board with a spoon in his right hand, while ketchup and spice jars stay stationary on the right. [144-146] A cross-fade transition shows the chef gesturing with open hands over the board holding toasted baguette slices, with the sauce bowl to his right and a bowl of raw ground meat appearing on the left. Other toasted slices remain arranged on a wooden cutting board in front of him. To the left, the tub of white substance sits on the counter. Nearby, bowls containing meat and sauce are visible. The chef continues spreading the substance on the single slice while the rest of the bread waits on the board.

Holding the bread in his left hand, which is topped with a white spread, he uses a utensil in his right hand to scoop reddish-brown sauce from a clear glass bowl. He proceeds to spread the sauce evenly over the white layer on the bread. The kitchen background includes wooden cabinets and stainless steel appliances.

input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).
